data.title: Hexagon's Spin: Core & Main Plunges 2.49%
Trump Retaliates: U.S. Boosts Tariffs on Canada Amid Trade Tensions
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has responded to Canada's retaliatory tariffs on U.S. goods, stating that the U.S. will immediately increase its reciprocal tariffs by the same amount. This move comes in response to Canada's decision to impose tariffs on a range of U.S. products, including steel, aluminum, and consumer goods.

The U.S. had previously imposed tariffs on Canadian steel and aluminum in 2018, citing national security concerns. Canada retaliated with its own tariffs on U.S. goods, which were later lifted after the U.S. and Canada reached a trade agreement. However, the U.S. has now reinstated its tariffs on Canadian steel and aluminum, prompting Canada to impose new retaliatory tariffs.

Trump's announcement on Twitter has been met with criticism from some quarters, with some arguing that the U.S. is escalating a trade war with one of its closest allies. However, others have suggested that the U.S. is simply responding to Canada's actions and that the two countries will ultimately reach a mutually beneficial agreement.

The impact of the tariffs on the U.S. and Canadian economies remains to be seen. Some economists have suggested that the tariffs could lead to job losses and higher prices for consumers in both countries. However, others have argued that the tariffs could help to protect domestic industries and create jobs in the long run.

In any case, the situation is likely to remain fluid in the coming weeks and months, as the U.S. and Canada continue to negotiate over trade and tariffs. Both countries have much to gain from a strong and mutually beneficial trading relationship, and it is in their interests to work together to resolve their differences.

data.title: Bitcoin & Friends: March's Crypto Showdown
data.description:
March is shaping up to be a significant month for Bitcoin, Ether, XRP, Solana, Cardano, and Shiba Inu, with several key events and developments on the horizon. As the cryptocurrency market continues to evolve, investors and traders are keeping a close eye on these events, which have the potential to shape the market's trajectory in the coming weeks.

Stablecoins are a major focus this month, with two crucial events set to clarify their role and significance in the United States. On March 6th, the CFTC and the White House will host a CEO forum to discuss the importance of stablecoins as collateral in the futures market and the position of tokenized assets in the broader market. Additionally, the Senate Banking Committee is scheduled to vote on the GENIUS Act stablecoin bill from February 10th to 14th, 2025. These events could influence market stability, with investors and traders reacting bullishly or bearishly depending on the outcomes.

The first White House Crypto Summit with President Donald Trump is scheduled for March 7th. Prominent crypto CEOs, founders, and investors are expected to attend, and with Trump having made pro-crypto remarks recently, market participants are optimistic about the weeks ahead.

On March 21st, the US Securities and Exchanges Commission (SEC) will host a roundtable to spearhead crypto regulation and policymaking conversations. This follows the regulator's integration of crypto task force members to discuss solutions to existing crypto regulatory concerns.

Historical patterns suggest that March is one of the strongest months for Bitcoin, with a 13.12% return for market players. As of this report, the global crypto market cap stands at $2.8 trillion, with a 5.82% decline in total market value over the past day. Despite this recent decline, the market reflects a bullish takeover, and investors are eagerly anticipating the developments that March has in store.

data.title: Trade Tensions Spark CEO Warning: Buckle Up for Uncertainty
data.description:
Carlyle Group CEO Harvey Schwartz has warned investors to brace themselves for uncertainty stemming from escalating trade tensions, as the stock market reverses gains made during the so-called "Trump rally." In a recent interview, Schwartz expressed concerns about the potential impact of tariffs on global economic growth and inflation.

Schwartz's remarks come as the U.S. and China engage in a protracted trade dispute, with both sides imposing tariffs on billions of dollars' worth of goods. The escalating tensions have sparked fears of a full-blown trade war, which could disrupt global supply chains and dampen economic growth.

The carlyle CEO is not alone in his concerns. Many economists and investors have sounded the alarm on the potential consequences of a prolonged trade war. Some have warned that the increased costs of imported goods could lead to sustained inflation, as Schwartz suggested.

Meanwhile, the stock market has been volatile in recent weeks, with investors grappling with uncertainty over the outcome of the trade negotiations. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, for example, has swung between gains and losses, wiping out much of the progress made during the Trump administration's early days.

The Trump rally, which began shortly after Donald Trump's election in 2016, was fueled by optimism about the president's economic policies, including tax cuts and deregulation. However, the rally has since lost steam, as investors have grown increasingly concerned about the potential fallout from the trade war.

Schwartz's call for investors to "buckle up" highlights the challenges posed by the current trade landscape. As the U.S. and China continue to negotiate, investors will likely remain on edge, waiting for signs of progress or further escalation.

data.title: Tesla's Boardroom Tension: Denholm's Loyalty Under Scrutiny Amid Stock Slide
data.description:
Tesla's stock price has been on a downward trajectory, and now, a key figure in the company's boardroom, Robyn Denholm, has added to the pressure. Denholm, a long-time supporter of Elon Musk, has been a significant presence in the company's governance, serving as the chair of the board of directors since 2018.

Denholm's role has been crucial in navigating tesla through various challenges, including regulatory issues and production setbacks. However, as the company's stock price continues to decline, her support for Musk has come under scrutiny. Some investors and analysts have questioned whether Denholm's loyalty to Musk is clouding her judgment and hindering the company's ability to make necessary changes.

Tesla's stock price has been volatile in recent months, with a significant drop in early 2022. The company's valuation has been impacted by a variety of factors, including supply chain disruptions, geopolitical tensions, and increased competition in the electric vehicle market. Despite these challenges, Tesla remains one of the most valuable automakers in the world.

Denholm's role in Tesla's boardroom has been a source of controversy in the past. In 2018, she was appointed as the chair of the board, replacing Musk, who was forced to step down due to regulatory issues. Denholm's appointment was seen as a way to strengthen Tesla's governance and provide a more independent voice in the boardroom. However, some critics have argued that Denholm has not been assertive enough in her role and has allowed Musk to maintain too much control over the company.

As Tesla's stock price continues to decline, there is growing pressure on the company's board of directors to take action. Some investors have called for a shake-up in the boardroom, arguing that the current leadership has not done enough to address the company's challenges. Denholm's role in this process will be crucial, as she will need to balance her loyalty to Musk with the need to make tough decisions for the good of the company.

Tesla's future remains uncertain, but one thing is clear: the company's boardroom dynamics will play a significant role in shaping its trajectory. As the company faces new challenges and opportunities, Denholm and the rest of the board will need to work together to ensure that Tesla remains a leader in the electric vehicle market. data.title: IP Shares Plummet 4.9%: Earnings Surprise?
Apple's Legal Battle: A Pivotal Moment for Privacy and Security
data.description:
Apple Inc. has launched an appeal against a UK government order demanding access to encrypted user data, marking a significant turning point in the ongoing debate between privacy and national security. The Financial Times reports that the tech giant is challenging the Investigatory Powers Act of 2016, which allows British authorities to compel companies to provide backdoor access to encrypted communications.



The UK government's demand for a backdoor access to iCloud data has raised concerns among privacy advocates and tech experts alike. apple, which has long championed user privacy and security, has refused to comply with the order, stating that it would be "wrong to weaken security for hundreds of millions of law-abiding customers so that it will also be weaker for the very few who pose a threat." The company's general counsel, Kate Adams, made this statement in a 2016 letter to the UK government.



Apple's decision to appeal the UK government's order highlights the delicate balance between national security and individual privacy. The company's commitment to user privacy has been a key factor in its global success, with the iPhone maker generating over $365 billion in revenue in 2021 alone. However, the UK government's demand for backdoor access to encrypted data could set a dangerous precedent for other governments to follow, potentially undermining the security and privacy of users worldwide.

The legal and regulatory challenges Apple faces in appealing the UK government's order are significant. The Investigatory Powers Act of 2016, which forms the basis of the UK government's demand, allows for penalties and even imprisonment for non-compliance. Additionally, the secrecy surrounding Technical Capability Notices (TCNs) makes it difficult for Apple to challenge the order publicly or seek support from users, other companies, or the public.



Apple's appeal could have far-reaching implications for the broader privacy landscape, both in the UK and globally. If the company succeeds in overturning the UK government's order, it could send a strong message to other governments that user privacy and security should not be compromised in the name of national security. However, if Apple's appeal fails, it could set a dangerous precedent for other governments to demand similar access to user data, potentially leading to a global rollback of encryption standards.

In conclusion, Apple's legal battle against the UK government's order for backdoor access to encrypted data is a pivotal moment for privacy and security in the digital age. The outcome of this appeal could have significant implications for users worldwide, as well as for the broader tech industry. As Apple fights to protect user privacy and security, it is essential for governments to strike a balance between national security and individual rights, ensuring that the interests of all citizens are protected.

data.title: Uranium Energy Corp. (UEC) Stock Plunges: What Happened?
data.description:
Uranium Energy Corp. (UEC) stock experienced a significant decline on Monday, with a loss of 23.2% over the past four weeks. This sharp drop has raised concerns among investors, prompting questions about the factors contributing to the stock's performance. By examining the company's fundamentals, industry trends, and geopolitical events, we can gain insights into the recent developments and their potential impact on UEC's long-term outlook.



One of the primary factors contributing to UEC's stock price decline is the market dislocation in energy, as mentioned by the company's CEO, Amir Adnani, on CNBC Television. This market dislocation could indicate that the energy market is experiencing significant volatility or uncertainty, which may have contributed to the stock's decline. Additionally, the Mining - Miscellaneous industry, to which uec belongs, has a Zacks Industry Rank of 163, putting it in the bottom 36% of all 250+ industries. This suggests that the industry as a whole may be facing challenges or underperforming, which could contribute to the company's stock price decline.



Another factor contributing to UEC's stock price decline is the significant decline in the company's revenue. In 2024, UEC's revenue was $224,000, a decrease of -99.86% compared to the previous year's $164.39 million. This substantial drop in revenue could be a concern for investors, as it suggests a significant decrease in the company's sales and profitability. Furthermore, the increased selling pressure on UEC's stock, with a loss of 23.2% over the past four weeks, indicates that there has been too much selling pressure, which would drive the stock price down.



Despite the recent stock price decline, analysts maintain a bullish outlook on UEC's future prospects. According to 2 analysts, the average rating for UEC stock is "Strong Buy," with a 12-month stock price forecast of $10.63, indicating a 105.21% increase from the latest price. This positive sentiment is likely driven by the company's strategic initiatives, such as the acquisition of Rio Tinto's Sweetwater Plant and Wyoming uranium assets, the completion of the initial economic assessment for the Roughrider Project, and the increase in licensed production capacity at the Irigaray Processing Plant. Additionally, the company's focus on uranium demand and nuclear energy in the 'Age of AI' may contribute to its positive outlook.



In conclusion, the recent decline in Uranium Energy Corp.'s stock price can be attributed to several factors, including market dislocation in energy, the company's revenue decline, and increased selling pressure. However, analysts maintain a bullish outlook on the company's future prospects, driven by strategic initiatives and the potential for growth in the uranium market. As the company continues to execute on its strategic plans and navigate the challenges in the energy market, investors should closely monitor UEC's performance and consider the long-term benefits of its strategic initiatives.

data.title: Bitcoin's March Surge: Bullish Trends and Upside Potential
data.description:
Bitcoin's March Outlook: Bullish Patterns and Upside Potential

As March begins, Bitcoin has successfully closed February above the $80,000 price mark, sparking optimism among key figures in the crypto community. Observing several positive trends, analysts have expressed their bullish expectations for the apex cryptocurrency in the coming month.

Pseudonymous crypto trader and investor DaanCrypto highlights Bitcoin's potential for a full comeback, stating that the upside potential will be more attractive to sidelined capital. With Bitcoin at $80K, the risk/reward ratio is notably promising, as assets are 20% cheaper. Long-term holders are expected to increase their investments, while a new entry point opens up for newbies.

Analyst Miles Deutscher affirms the bullish sentiments, reminding the market that March has historically been an excellent month for Bitcoin. The asset typically averages a 13.12% gain around this time, making it the third strongest month of the year after October and November.

After sustaining heavy losses on February 28, Bitcoin has since recovered and is trading at a press time price of $85,412, a few thousand dollars away from its all-time high of $109,114. The asset has successfully secured 10% in daily gains, with daily trading volume soaring up by 193.33%.

data.title: Israel Unveils Digital Shekel Design: A New Era of Money
data.description:
The Bank of Israel has taken a significant step towards exploring the next generation of money by releasing the preliminary design for its planned central bank digital currency (CBDC), the Digital Shekel (DS). This detailed proposal provides insights into the ecosystem, functionalities, technical architecture, and regulatory considerations that the bank is considering. This move aligns with a broader trend of central banks worldwide exploring CBDCs as a means of updating their financial systems and improving payment networks.

The Digital Shekel aims to be user-friendly and inclusive, with the potential to provide cheaper, more innovative means of transaction, better payment infrastructure, strengthened privacy, and a means to fight the "black economy." The design emphasizes inclusivity, ensuring that the CBDC is available to all segments of the population, including children, foreigners, businesses, public institutions, and financial entities.

The preliminary design describes several key features of the digital shekel, including offline functionality, interoperability, and immediate clearing and settlement of payments. If implemented effectively, these features could significantly improve the user experience and expand the adoption of the Digital Shekel.

The Bank of Israel will adopt a two-tier model, issuing and supervising the new digital shekel while the private sector, including Digital Shekel Payment Service Providers (DSPPs), will onboard users and provide customer-facing services. The bank is also seeking technology and business input through the "Digital Shekel Challenge" to ensure the CBDC is practical for everyday use.

Israel is taking a gradual and cautious approach to the implementation of the Digital Shekel. A final decision on issuing the CBDC will be taken after 2026, allowing the Bank of Israel time to conduct thorough research, gather public input, and address regulatory issues. The bank is also focusing on interoperability, ensuring that the digital shekel can be used in conjunction with other payment systems and digital asset networks.

The privacy of end-users is a significant aspect of the CBDC plan. The design ensures that no information about users' balances or transactions within the digital shekel will be available to the Bank of Israel or any central entity. Only PSPs will hold user data, and certain transactions below specified limits may be made anonymously.

The proposed ecosystem includes various entities with distinct roles, such as the Bank of Israel as the sole issuer and system manager, DSPPs for user onboarding and access technology, funding institutions for converting funds between accounts and DS, and additional service providers

data.title: Bitcoin Stabilizes Near $83K as S&P 500 Recovers
data.description:
Bitcoin's price has stabilized near $83,000 as investors keep an eye on the S&P 500 recovery. The recent volatility in Bitcoin (BTC) highlights the tendency of markets to overreact, especially in situations that can escalate, such as trade wars. The 6.5% drop in the S&P 500 since its all-time high on Feb. 19 might seem minor in absolute terms, but the potential earnings impact is more significant. However, derivatives markets suggest that Bitcoin's dip below $83,000 should be short-lived.

Traders tend to sell off assets when they sense a recession coming. Presently, investors are moving into cash and short-term government bonds. This shift explains why the US 2-year Treasury yield recently hit its lowest level in five months. Traders are willing to accept lower yields, which shows strong buying interest.

US 2-year Treasury yield (left) vs. Bitcoin/USD (right). Source: TradingView / Cointelegraph

Bitcoin derivatives markets held firm despite the 16% correction since the rejection at $99,500 on Feb. 21, indicating that whales and market makers do not expect further declines. More importantly, even if the much-anticipated United States strategic digital asset reserves fail to secure congressional approval, there is still strong political momentum at the state level, keeping the initiatives alive.

Bitcoin 2-month futures annualized premium. Source: Laevitas.ch

Bitcoin futures have maintained a stable 6.5% annualized premium (basis rate) over spot markets as of March 4, unchanged from the prior week. This metric remains within the neutral 5% to 10% range observed over the past four weeks—a clear indication that professional traders are unfazed by recent volatility, showing confidence in market stability.

Bitcoin 30-day options delta skew (put-call). Source: Laevitas.ch

The Bitcoin options 25% delta skew (put-call) stood at 4% on March 4, reflecting balanced pricing between put (sell) and call (buy) options. Given the failed attempt to reclaim the $94,000 support on March 3, the low demand for protective puts signals resilience among investors.

data.title: Trump's Tariffs: Walmart's 2% Plunge
Southwest Airlines: Navigating Headwinds at the J.P. Morgan Industrials Conference
data.description:
Southwest Airlines (LUV) is set to present at the J.P. Morgan Industrials Conference, offering investors a glimpse into the company's financial performance and strategic outlook. As the airline industry continues to face headwinds, southwest airlines has been proactive in addressing the challenges and positioning itself for long-term growth. In this article, we will explore the key factors driving the recent revision in Southwest Airlines' financial guidance and discuss how the company's strategic response positions it for long-term success.



The recent revision in Southwest Airlines' financial guidance, particularly the decrease in expected Revenue per Available seat Mile (RASM) and the increase in economic fuel costs per gallon, can be attributed to several key factors. Higher than expected completion factors in February and March led to a decrease in the expected RASM, with the company now anticipating a flat to 2% increase year-over-year, down from the previously forecasted 2.5% to 4.5% rise. Additionally, lower than anticipated close-in leisure passenger volume contributed to the revision in expected RASM. The airline also reported an increase in economic fuel costs per gallon, with the first quarter estimate now between $2.95 and $3.00, higher than the previous estimate of $2.70 to $2.80. Shifts in the timing of various expenses, including salaries, wages, and maintenance, contributed to a slight increase in the expected rise of CASM-X, which is now projected to be about 6% year-over-year.

In response to these revised financial trends, Southwest Airlines has taken several strategic steps to position itself for long-term growth and profitability. The company has paused hiring for several workgroups, including Pilots and Flight Attendants, to manage its headcount and expenses more effectively. This decision is expected to result in a lower headcount year-over-year, which should help the company control its operational costs and improve its financial performance. Additionally, Southwest Airlines has adjusted its capacity and schedules in response to the reduced aircraft delivery expectations from Boeing. The company is now set to receive 46 Boeing 737-8 aircraft in 2024, down from the previously expected 79 737 MAX aircraft deliveries. This reduction, along with the current certification status of the 737-7 model, has prompted the airline to revise its capacity and schedules, particularly for the latter half of the year. This strategic move allows Southwest Airlines to better align its capacity with demand, ensuring that it can maintain profitability even in the face of changing market conditions.

Furthermore, Southwest Airlines has committed to providing updated full-year 2024 guidance on April 25, 2024, when it reports its first-quarter financial results. This proactive approach to communication demonstrates the company's dedication to transparency and accountability, which should help build investor confidence and support long-term growth.

In conclusion, Southwest Airlines' strategic response to the revised financial trends, including pausing hiring, adjusting capacity and schedules, and committing to updated guidance, positions the company to better navigate the current market conditions and maintain its long-term growth and profitability. As the airline industry continues to evolve, Southwest Airlines remains committed to adapting its business model to ensure its ongoing success. Investors should closely monitor the company's performance and strategic initiatives as it presents at the J.P. Morgan Industrials Conference and beyond.

data.title: US Jobless Claims Rise to 242,000 – Labor Market Concerns Worry Investors
data.description:
Converted Markdown

The Bottom Line Up Front:New unemployment filings surged to 242,000 this week – the highest level since December 2024 and well above Wall Street’s 221,000 forecast. This surprise jump signals growing stress in the job market, forcing investors to rethink bets on Federal Reserve rate cuts and resilient corporate earnings. Here’s what you need to know.

Why This Matters Right Now

The labor market has been the bedrock of the U.S. economy’s strength, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits even as inflation stayed stubborn. But cracks are showing:

  1. Fed Policy Shift? The Fed has held rates steady since July 2024, but rising job losses could push them to cut rates sooner to prevent a deeper downturn.
  2. Recession Watch: The Sahm Rule – which flags recessions when unemployment spikes – is flashing yellow. Today’s data brings us closer to that trigger.
  3. Portfolio Impact: Stocks slid on the news, while bonds rallied as traders sought safety. Sector winners/losers are shifting fast.

Breaking Down the Jobless Claims Data

What Are Jobless Claims?

Jobless claims track how many Americans file for unemployment benefits each week. It’s split into two numbers:

  • Initial claims: First-time filers (this week: 242,000)
  • Continuing claims: People still receiving benefits (this week: 1.87 million)

Think of it as a real-time pulse check on layoffs. When claims rise, it means more people are losing jobs – and that often hints at bigger economic trouble ahead14.

This Week’s Surprise Spike

MetricThis WeekLast WeekEconomist ForecastInitial Claims242,000220,000221,0004-Week Average224,000215,500–

Key Takeaway: Claims haven’t been this high since the holiday season. The 4-week average (which smooths weekly swings) also hit a 3-month high45.

What’s Driving the Layoffs?

  • Corporate Cutbacks: Tech, retail, and manufacturing companies are trimming staff to protect profits as sales slow.
  • Government Layoffs: Thousands of federal workers lost jobs under a new “efficiency program,” but these numbers aren’t fully reflected in today’s report5.
  • Weather Woes: Severe winter storms forced temporary shutdowns in multiple states, pushing claims higher4.

Investor Implications: 3 Ways This Shakes Markets

1. Fed Rate Cut Odds Rise (But It’s Complicated)

The Fed wants to see inflation fall and employment stay strong. With prices still rising faster than their 2% target (CPI was 3.1% in January), today’s weak jobs data puts them in a tough spot:

  • Dovish Scenario: If unemployment keeps rising, expect rate cuts by June to stimulate hiring.
  • Hawkish Reality: Services inflation (think healthcare, rents) remains hot, which could delay cuts despite job market pain24.

Market Move: After the report, traders priced in a 40% chance of a June rate cut – up from 28% last week.

2. Sector Rotations Accelerate

  • Losers: Cyclical stocks tied to economic growth – like industrials (‑0.8%) and consumer discretionary (‑1.1%) – sold off.
  • Winners: “Safe haven” assets like utilities (+1.2%) and gold (+0.9%) gained as investors hedged recession risks45.

3. Bond Market Sees Mixed Signals

  • Shorter-Term Bonds: 2-year Treasury yields fell to 4.35% as rate-cut hopes grew.
  • Longer-Term Bonds: 10-year yields dropped to 4.05%, reflecting worries about slower growth ahead45.

The Big Debate: Blip or Breakdown?

Bull Case: Temporary Glitch

  • Weather and seasonal adjustments distorted the numbers.
  • Continuing claims (people staying unemployed) barely moved.
  • Job openings still outnumber unemployed workers 1.4-to-1.

Bear Case: Trend Shift

  • Layoffs are spreading beyond tech to “Main Street” sectors.
  • The 4-week claims average has risen 11% since January.
  • Consumer confidence just hit a 9-month low – a red flag for spending.

Your Action Plan

  1. Revisit Defense: Boost exposure to sectors like healthcare, utilities, and consumer staples that weather downturns better.
  2. Watch the Fed: The March 20 FOMC meeting could hint at policy shifts – listen for any mention of labor market risks.
  3. Next Data Drop: The March 8 jobs report will show if unemployment rose past 4%. A move to 4.2% would trigger the Sahm Rule’s recession warning45.

Bottom Line for InvestorsToday’s jobless claims report doesn’t guarantee a recession, but it’s a warning sign that the economy’s “immune system” is weakening. With stocks near record highs and bond markets pricing in rate cuts, positioning your portfolio for volatility is key. Stick with quality companies (strong balance sheets, steady cash flows) and keep some dry powder to buy dips if the jobs slump deepens.

⁂

data.title: What to Expect from Trump's Congress Speech?
data.description:
President Donald Trump is set to deliver a powerful speech before Congress on Tuesday at 9 PM Eastern Time, centered around the theme of MAGA. This address will provide insight into the agenda of the new administration. 

Here's what to expect from Trump's speech:

Addressing High Costs and Tariffs

The U.S. continues to struggle with high inflation. Since taking office, Trump has blamed high prices on his predecessor, Joe Biden, but has yet to propose concrete measures to lower costs. Some Republicans worry that inflation could pose a political risk, and Trump's tariff hikes may further drive up prices.

In his speech, Trump is expected to outline his administration's approach to tackling inflation and justify the necessity of tariffs.

Immigration Policy: Historic Mass Deportation Plan

Trump is advancing the largest illegal immigrant deportation effort in U.S. history. The Department of Homeland Security and the Department of Defense have requested additional funding to deport millions of undocumented immigrants. However, Trump and his top officials remain dissatisfied with the current pace of arrests by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE).

Trump is likely to reiterate his commitment to strengthening border security and push Congress for additional funding to build more border barriers. Meanwhile, Democratic-led states like California and New York have vowed to challenge his policies and protect undocumented immigrants.

Ukraine and Middle East Policy

Last Friday, Trump had a heated exchange with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky at the White House over the ongoing Ukraine war, drawing global attention.

Trump's shifting stance on Ukraine is seen as a pivotal moment for U.S.-Europe relations. The future of U.S. military aid to Ukraine remains uncertain, and European nations fear Trump may reduce or even cut off support.

In his speech, Trump is expected to discuss his approach to facilitating a ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine, as well as possible reductions in funding and military assistance. He may also emphasize Europe's need to take on greater responsibility, pressuring the UK, France, and Germany to increase their aid to Ukraine. Additionally, U.S.-Ukraine mineral agreements may be mentioned.

Trump's remarks could set a new direction for U.S. policy on Ukraine and have significant geopolitical implications.

Meanwhile, he is also expected to address his strategy for ending the ongoing conflict between Israel and Hamas. Since returning to the White House, Trump has met with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, the first foreign leader to visit him in Washington. Trump has repeatedly stated that the U.S. will exert control over Gaza.

Government Restructuring: Mass Federal Layoffs

The Trump administration is undergoing the largest federal restructuring in U.S. history, including the dismissal of thousands of federal employees and a reduction in government agencies.

The restructuring is being spearheaded by the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), led by billionaire entrepreneur Elon Musk, aiming to streamline operations and cut redundant expenses. However, DOGE's aggressive approach has raised concerns among voters, with many writing to their congressional representatives questioning Musk's decisions. Additionally, outsourcing government functions to private firms has sparked debates over data security risks.

Musk is expected to attend the speech as a guest in the congressional gallery. Trump may use his speech to defend the department's reforms and reassure moderate Republicans and independents, though strong criticism from Democrats is anticipated.

data.title: "CUDIS Revolutionizes Crypto Mental Health: First On-Chain Emotion Tracker Launched"
data.description:
CUDIS, a pioneering Web3 health-tech platform, has launched the first onchain emotion tracker integrated with Backpack Wallet, a leading Solana and Ethereum-based wallet. This novel dApp, Energy Journaling, is designed to help users track their emotions and energy levels daily, promoting mental well-being in the fast-paced world of crypto.

The emotion tracker is integrated directly into users' wallets, enabling them to log their moods, track energy levels, and gain insights into their mental state while earning rewards for prioritizing self-care. This feature is made more accessible through the integration with Backpack Wallet, which is the first crypto wallet to support onchain emotion journaling.

CUDIS is a leader in decentralized health technology, empowering users with onchain wellness monitoring, AI-driven coaching, and data ownership. The emotion journal offers a unique way for users to engage in self-awareness practices while benefiting from a tokenized reward system.

Users log their emotional and energy levels daily via the CUDIS dApp within Backpack Wallet. Blockchain technology ensures transparency and security, allowing individuals to store and verify their data autonomously. By maintaining a journaling streak, users earn CUDIS points, which can be redeemed for in-app rewards such as SALUS points and raffle entries. Over time, AI-driven insights provide personalized recommendations based on emotional fluctuations, fostering a more balanced and informed approach to mental well-being.

CUDIS plans to announce additional wallet integration partners throughout 2025, expanding the reach of this innovative mental wellness tool across the blockchain ecosystem.

data.title: Japan's Crypto Boom: 11M Accounts, Tight Regulation
data.description:
Binance CEO Richard Teng has praised Japan's approach to cryptocurrency regulation, highlighting the country's commitment to investor protection and transparency. Speaking at the Global Financial Technology Network Forum in Japan, Teng noted that Japan had 11 million cryptocurrency accounts in 2024, underscoring the need for robust regulations to ensure long-term stability and security in the market.

The Financial Services Agency (FSA) of Japan is responsible for overseeing the country's crypto regulations, enforcing the Payment Services Act (PSA) and Financial Instruments and Exchange Act (FIEA). Self-regulatory organizations such as the Japan Virtual Currency Exchange Association (JVCEA) play a crucial role in implementing investor protection, security, and transparency-oriented laws. The FSA has recently warned several exchanges, including Bybit, KuCoin, Bitget, Bitcastle, and mexc global, for operating without proper licenses.

Japan is also pushing forward with new regulatory changes for crypto assets and stablecoins. Yufu Shigeyuki, Director of Planning and Market Bureau at the FSA, has proposed recommendations based on discussions at a recent Financial System Council meeting. These reforms include a domestic asset holding order to enhance investor protections, ensuring that crypto exchanges maintain distinct asset separation. Additionally, the proposal aims to separate crypto broking companies from other business undertakings and provide stablecoin issuers greater freedom in managing trust-supported assets. Future amendments to the Trust Business Act and Payment Services Act will further solidify these improvements.

Teng's endorsement of Japan's regulatory approach is a testament to Binance's global pursuit of regulation. His admiration for Japan's accomplishments suggests that the country may serve as an inspiration for others seeking to strike the ideal balance between innovation and investor protection. As Japan continues to develop its crypto regulations, it remains a key player in the global regulatory landscape.

Japan's regulation has positioned it as a model for innovation and investor protection in the cryptocurrency industry. With increasing adoption of cryptocurrency, Teng believes that other countries can look to Japan's model as a template for creating clear and functional regulations.

data.title: On Holding's Q4 2024: Contradictions in Brand Strategy, Apparel Growth, and Americas Expansion
data.description:
These are the key contradictions discussed in On Holding's latest 2024 Q4 earnings call, specifically including: Brand Awareness and Marketing Strategy, Apparel Growth and Strategy, and Americas Growth:



Revenue and Growth:
- On Holdings AG reported net sales of CHF2.32 billion for the full year 2024, with a constant currency growth rate of over 33%.
- The company exceeded its expectations in both top-line growth and profitability, driven by strong brand positioning and strategic product offerings.

Premium Brand and Apparel Expansion:
- On's apparel sales reached over CHF100 million, contributing to a significant increase in their apparel share.
- This growth is attributed to elevated apparel collections, strategic partnerships, and the expansion of On's retail presence in major city centers, which has enhanced brand visibility and consumer engagement.

Running and Apparel Sales Performance:
- The top three running franchises, Cloudmonster, Cloudsurfer, and Cloudrunner, experienced growth between 60% and 140% during 2024.
- Growth in the running segment was driven by new product introductions, strategic partnerships, and increased demand from younger customers.

Retail Expansion and Market Penetration:
- On opened 19 new retail stores in iconic locations during 2024, contributing to a retail space of over 10,000 square meters.
- This expansion strategy, particularly in Europe and Asia, has enhanced brand presence, driven by a focus on premium retail experiences and market penetration through global events and partnerships.

data.title: Société Foncière Lyonnaise and Inmobiliaria Colonial: A Merger for Prime Real Estate Dominance
data.description:
Société Foncière Lyonnaise (SFL) and Inmobiliaria Colonial have signed a merger agreement, marking a significant step towards creating a leading prime real estate platform in Europe. The proposed cross-border merger, approved by both boards of directors, aims to simplify the group's structure and strengthen its position in the prime commercial real estate market.

The exchange ratio has been set at 13 Colonial shares for 1 sfl share, representing a significant premium on net asset value (NTA) parity. Additionally, an exit price of €77.5 per SFL share has been determined, which will be adjusted by the amount of SFL's dividend to be approved by its general meeting prior to the merger. The exchange ratio and exit price have been determined based on a multi-criteria analysis reflecting commonly used valuation methods.

The merger is expected to generate substantial synergies and cost savings, enabling SFL shareholders to remain invested in the value creation potential of the group with significantly enhanced liquidity or to access full liquidity at a price corresponding to the multi-criteria valuation of SFL. The combined entity will have a diversified portfolio of commercial real estate assets in prime locations across Europe, with a total market value exceeding €19 billion and a total area of over 1.1 million square meters.

However, the proposed merger raises potential antitrust concerns, as it would result in a significant increase in market concentration in the prime commercial real estate market in Europe. The combined entity would have a dominant position in the market, potentially leading to higher prices and reduced competition. The European Commission will need to conduct a thorough assessment of the merger's effects on competition in the relevant markets and may impose remedies to mitigate any negative effects.



In conclusion, the signing of the agreement to merge Société Foncière Lyonnaise into Inmobiliaria Colonial is a significant event in the European prime real estate market. The merger is expected to create a leading platform in the market, with substantial synergies and cost savings. However, potential antitrust concerns must be addressed to ensure that the merger does not negatively impact competition in the market. The European Commission will play a crucial role in assessing the merger's effects on competition and imposing remedies if necessary.

data.title: On Holding Races Ahead: Strong Q4 Results Keep Momentum High
data.description:
On Holding AG (NYSE: ONON) reported strong fourth-quarter earnings, exceeding expectations on both earnings per share (EPS) and revenue. The Swiss running shoe and apparel company delivered adjusted EPS of CHF 0.27, significantly outperforming analyst estimates of CHF 0.14. This marked a notable turnaround from the loss per share of CHF 0.08 reported a year ago. Revenue came in at CHF 606.6 million, representing a 36% year-over-year increase and surpassing the CHF 595.2 million forecasted by analysts.

Key Metrics and Performance Drivers

Beyond the top and bottom-line beats, on holding delivered strong growth across several key financial and operational metrics. Gross profit increased 39% year-over-year to CHF 376.8 million, exceeding the CHF 367 million estimate, while the gross margin reached a record-high 62.1%, beating expectations of 61.7%. Adjusted EBITDA came in at CHF 99.4 million, up 38% from the prior year and ahead of the CHF 97.2 million consensus estimate.

Regional performance was mixed, with strong growth in the EMEA and Asia-Pacific regions offsetting a slight miss in the Americas. EMEA net sales reached CHF 147.4 million, well above the CHF 136.5 million forecast, while Asia-Pacific surged to CHF 74.1 million, surpassing estimates of CHF 61.1 million. Americas net sales, however, came in at CHF 385.1 million, missing the CHF 397.3 million estimate but still growing at a solid 34% year-over-year pace in constant currency terms.

Direct-to-consumer (DTC) sales were a standout, growing 43% year-over-year to CHF 296.2 million, outpacing wholesale sales growth of 29%. The shift toward dtc helped drive overall margin expansion, with the channel mix reaching 48.8% of total sales, an increase of 260 basis points from the prior year.

Comments on Tariffs and Supply Chain Strategy

On Holding provided some insights into the potential impact of recently enacted U.S. tariffs, though the company appears well-insulated from immediate disruptions. In its 20-F filing with the SEC, On Holding acknowledged that tariffs could pose a headwind but noted that its supply chain is well diversified. Approximately 90% of its shoes and 60% of its apparel and accessories are sourced from Vietnam, while only 7% of its apparel and accessories come from China. Notably, On does not source any of its footwear from China, nor does it source goods from Canada or Mexico, shielding it from the latest round of U.S. tariffs on those regions.

Guidance and Future Outlook

For fiscal year 2025, On Holding provided guidance that was slightly below expectations. The company expects net sales of at least CHF 2.94 billion, just shy of the CHF 2.96 billion analyst consensus. Adjusted EBITDA margin is projected in the range of 17% to 17.5%, in line with expectations. Gross margin is expected to be around 60.5%, slightly below the 60.7% forecasted by analysts.

Management indicated that 2025 is off to a strong start, with the company expecting higher revenue growth in the first half of the year compared to the second half. This optimism is largely driven by the timing of product launches, including the Cloud 6, which debuted in February. Given On's track record of conservative guidance, investors may be encouraged by the possibility of an upward revision later in the year.

Market Reaction and Stock Performance

Following the earnings report, onon stock jumped nearly 7% in early trading before paring gains to 2.8%. Shares have been attempting to find support around the 200-day moving average after experiencing a steep decline in recent weeks. ONON stock hit a record high of $64.05 on January 31 but has since pulled back, declining 12.6% year-to-date through Monday’s close.

Despite the recent weakness, analysts remain optimistic about On Holding’s long-term trajectory. Raymond James reaffirmed a strong buy rating and a $68.00 price target, citing the company’s ability to consistently exceed revenue and profitability targets. On has a history of beating its own guidance, having outperformed initial forecasts for revenue growth, gross margin, and adjusted EBITDA margin in 2024.

Competitive Landscape and Industry Trends

The strong results come at a time when the broader athletic footwear and apparel industry is facing mixed demand trends. Competitors like Deckers Outdoor (DECK), which owns the Hoka brand, have seen significant stock declines in recent weeks, with DECK shares tumbling nearly 39% from their January highs. However, On Holding’s strong DTC growth, expanding gross margins, and continued brand momentum position it favorably in the current environment.

The company’s ability to capitalize on high-margin DTC sales, coupled with its strategic supply chain diversification, provides a competitive edge against industry headwinds. Looking ahead, key areas of focus for investors will include the reception of new product launches, the performance of On’s wholesale segment versus DTC, and any updates on potential currency headwinds or supply chain developments.

Conclusion

On Holding capped off 2024 with a strong Q4 performance, delivering record gross margins, robust sales growth, and an accelerating DTC mix. While 2025 guidance came in slightly below expectations, the company’s historical tendency to exceed forecasts provides room for upside. With continued product innovation, expansion in international markets, and resilience against tariff impacts, On Holding appears well-positioned for sustained growth in the year ahead. Investors will be closely watching the first half of 2025 to see if the company’s bullish outlook on early-year sales momentum holds up.
